bootstrap_constraints: No live network calls during deterministic bootstrap.
No sandbox credentials or model keys until human approval.
No unredacted secrets in committed artifacts.
All Factory references must be repo-relative.
Go toolchain must be pinned to 1.26.4 before implementation proceeds.
T1 must not add unpinned or floating dependencies.
Standalone binary is the primary distribution target; Homebrew follows after first binary; PyPI is not primary.
Repository delivery lifecycle baseline must exist before T3 product implementation starts.
Repository dev/architecture propagation baseline must exist before T3 product implementation starts.
proof_requirements: Validation reports must record command, exit status, and skipped-command reason when applicable.
Scope closure must map every MVP acceptance group to implemented, partial, missing, blocked, or deferred.
Live credential-dependent work must remain blocked until credential posture is approved.
Lifecycle-gated tasks must carry review, ship, post-merge, and PR lifecycle evidence or an explicit approved exception.
Scanner-gated tasks must carry CodeQL status evidence or an explicit approved exception.
CI lane, docs parity, output contract, release integrity, and provenance requirements must be represented in task evidence or approved exceptions.
Medium/high risk architecture work must carry systems-thinking, TDD/skipped-reason, ADR, performance, reliability, and fail-closed evidence as applicable.
Factory planning-skill alignment must be present in the execution plan, validation contract, and task packets.

Task packets must carry changelog/versioning intent, contract/API impact, architecture constraints, ADR posture, TDD-first evidence, cost/perf impact, chaos/failure hypothesis, and semantic invariants.
Plan drift after alignment must update context brief, execution plan, task packets, and validation contract before implementation continues.
Any task with public or executable contract impact must set adr_required=true and carry an ADR or decision-note expectation before closure.
Factory compatibility must be present in context brief, execution plan, validation contract, and task packets.
Active required_worker_chain values must use commit-push, not deprecated aliases such as ship-pr.
Task packets must carry explicit scope_exclusions, alignment_gate_ref, and plan_drift_policy_ref inherited from the execution plan.
Runtime pins must include toolchain_version, module_or_package_path, provider_policy, artifact_namespace, and live_work_policy before implementation starts.
Live eval MVP closure must cover OpenAI-compatible provider adapters, including custom base_url local endpoints, and Anthropic provider adapters when configured.
Coverage-gated tasks must carry make test-coverage evidence or an approved scoped coverage exception.

CI control-set evidence must prove required-check metadata, least-privilege workflow permissions, concurrency, timeouts, toolchain-pin source, owner-review coverage, action-ref posture, and branch-protection expectations for lifecycle, workflow, policy, scanner, or autonomous shipping work.
Version-slice coverage must map legacy v0.0, v0.1, and v0.2 internal slices to task refs, acceptance groups, and scope-closure entries before any full MVP completion claim.

Safety/corpus-ready evidence must preserve finding kind, proof strength, boundary status, security relevance, fix target, surface fingerprint, provider/model metadata when applicable, eval mode, and corpus_eligible false by default.
Safety/corpus-ready tasks must carry safety_corpus_ready_evidence_refs that cite docs/product/prd.md#safety-and-corpus-ready-evidence and the relevant acceptance-ledger item.
stop_conditions: implementation requires undeclared live credentials
implementation writes outside allowed paths
redaction confidence is unknown for persisted artifacts
tests pass while explicit PRD scope remains unrepresented in scope closure
CI/status, review, shipping, post-merge, or PR lifecycle evidence is missing for a lifecycle-gated task
CodeQL/security scanner evidence is missing for scanner-gated work
test-matrix refs, scanner gates, or architecture guidance refs are missing from a product task packet
CI lane refs are missing from a product task packet
docs parity, output contract, release integrity, or provenance evidence refs are missing for touched surfaces
systems-thinking, TDD, ADR, performance, reliability, or fail-closed refs are missing for touched architecture surfaces
Factory prd-to-plan or execution-compiler required fields are missing from the execution plan or a task packet
changelog/versioning intent, contract/API impact, ADR posture, TDD-first evidence, cost/perf impact, chaos/failure hypothesis, or semantic invariants are missing for a task

alignment drift changes runtime, distribution, provider, dependency, artifact namespace, credential, live-work, guide, or validation-contract decisions without updating context brief, execution plan, task packets, and validation contract
public or executable contract impact is declared while adr_required is false or missing
factory_compatibility is missing from context brief, execution plan, validation contract, or a task packet
active required_worker_chain contains a deprecated worker alias such as ship-pr
scope_exclusions, alignment_gate_ref, or plan_drift_policy_ref are missing from a task packet
runtime_pins omit toolchain_version, module_or_package_path, provider_policy, artifact_namespace, or live_work_policy
live eval provider work omits either OpenAI-compatible or Anthropic adapter coverage without approved scope exception
coverage policy refs or coverage evidence are missing from a product task packet
make test-coverage is below threshold without an approved scoped exception
required-check metadata, workflow permissions, concurrency, timeouts, owner-review coverage, action-ref posture, or branch-protection evidence is missing for CI/workflow/policy/lifecycle work
acceptance ledger item missing, unmapped, or closed only at group level
required MVP version slice v0.0, v0.1, or v0.2 lacks task, acceptance mapping, validation-contract, or scope-closure coverage
safety/corpus-ready acceptance items are missing from task packets, scope closure, validation contract, or delivery slices
normalized failure evidence omits corpus_eligible false by default or implies hosted telemetry/upload without approved scope change
live eval provider work bundles model weights or local inference-runtime payloads instead of using provider/base_url configuration

supervised_autoship_policy.required_when: A human supervisor explicitly oversees one factoryd autoship task from the Lumyn repo root.
supervised_autoship_policy.validation_rule: Supervisor evidence supplements, but never replaces, validation_report, work_proof_marker, ship_packet, pr_lifecycle_report, post_merge_report, scope closure, CI, and latest-head Codex review evidence.
blocked_if: supervisor is used without a single task_id
manual intervention bypasses factoryd lifecycle evidence
manual acceptance lacks explicit gate evidence
non-convergent repair is applied without recording blocker classification

runtime_pins.dependency_policy: standard library first; pinned Go modules only when a task proves need and records justification
runtime_pins.provider_policy: OpenAI-compatible HTTP including custom base_url local endpoints, and Anthropic Messages HTTP adapters; live provider endpoint work requires a complete model_provider_endpoint grant and no model key or network is used in deterministic bootstrap
runtime_pins.artifact_namespace: .factory/artifacts/ for Factory evidence; product artifacts use workflows/, cassettes/, runs/, baselines/, schemas/, and examples/
runtime_pins.live_work_policy: blocked until deterministic replay foundation passes and complete model_provider_endpoint, sandbox credential, or network grants unlock the relevant live work

release: reserved until standalone binary release packaging starts

engineering_policies.docs_parity: Behavior, flags, outputs, install paths, and workflow semantics update README, PRD, dev/architecture guides, and Factory planning artifacts together.
engineering_policies.output_contracts: Command-result envelope, exit codes, schemas, cassettes, evidence, reports, and run artifacts are product contracts.
engineering_policies.release_integrity: Standalone binary release tasks must define version, changelog, install, artifact-integrity, and UAT evidence before merge.
engineering_policies.provenance_evidence: Validation reports, work-proof markers, PR lifecycle reports, scanner status, and scope closure evidence remain repo-relative.

architecture_policies.systems_thinking: Medium/high risk tasks record state owner, feedback source, deletion blast radius, rollback/deletion test, and source-of-truth impact.
architecture_policies.tdd: Behavior changes require test-first evidence or structured skipped reason; schema/output changes prefer failing fixture or validator expectation first.
architecture_policies.adr_triggers: Boundary crossings, public contracts, failure classes, state model, performance, security, credential/network, and release posture changes require ADR or decision note.
architecture_policies.performance: Replay/eval/report hot paths and model-cost paths require budget notes and validation or approved exception.
architecture_policies.reliability: Retries, cleanup, persistence, filesystem mutation, redaction, live network, and provider work require hardening checks and failure-mode evidence.
architecture_policies.failure_semantics: Ambiguous proof, redaction, credential, stale cassette, undeclared control, or scanner state fails closed.

deprecated_worker_aliases.migration_behavior: block active task packets until required_worker_chain is migrated

drift_triggers: Factory contract, profile, skill inventory, or skill vocabulary changes that alter required fields, lifecycle workers, validation gates, or evidence expectations

mvp_eval_provider_adapters.sdk_policy: no heavy SDK dependency for MVP bootstrap; use thin HTTP adapters unless a later task justifies a pinned SDK
mvp_eval_provider_adapters.network_policy: blocked until deterministic replay foundation passes and a complete model_provider_endpoint grant approves provider identity, provider model, endpoint/base_url, credential environment, budget posture, redaction posture, and network allowlist

runtime.approval_posture: human approval required for live credentials, high-risk tasks, and merge
runtime.credential_posture: no ambient secrets during deterministic MVP bootstrap
runtime.network_posture: offline by default until live sandbox/model work is approved

factoryd_runtime_requirements.failure_behavior: block dispatch and emit repair evidence when runner-ready fields, path policy, validation commands, or lifecycle evidence are missing

coverage_policy.exception_policy: coverage misses block promotion unless the task records an approved scoped exception with owner, reason, expiry or follow-up task, and compensating validation evidence

acceptance_criteria: FDN-001: Create the Go CLI skeleton, command-result JSON envelope, exit-code constants, command dispatch, and repo-local validation baseline needed before product commands are implemented.
FDN-002: Define initial workflow, command-result, proof-strength, cassette, evidence-event, state-binding, redaction-config, and related schema contracts before feature implementation.

FDN-003: Normalized result and failure contracts preserve finding kind, proof strength, boundary status, security relevance, fix target, surface fingerprint, provider/model metadata when applicable, eval mode, and default corpus_eligible: false posture.

FDL-001: Repository CI validation, required-check metadata, workflow hardening, owner review, action-ref posture, branch protection evidence, and PR lifecycle evidence are established before product implementation continues.

FDAP-001: Repo dev guide, architecture guide, 12-level test matrix, coverage policy, security scanner posture, docs/output/release/provenance rules, and architecture boundary rules are propagated into executable planning.
FR1: FR1: Product Surface Under Test - Lumyn must frame the customer's API/docs surface as the system under test and agents as probes.
FR2: FR2: Local-First Operation - The MVP must work without a hosted Lumyn account.
FR3: FR3: OpenAPI And Local Docs Intake - Lumyn must ingest an OpenAPI file and local docs folder.
FR4: FR4: Agent-Relevant Source Checks - Lumyn must run structural checks that affect agent tool choice, validation, auth handling, retries, and workflow proof.
FR5: FR5: Workflow Contract Schema - Lumyn must define a versioned YAML workflow contract with goal, expected outcome, validators, state bindings, context, constraints, and cleanup.
FR6: FR6: Recording As Authoring - Lumyn must record known-good HTTP traffic and draft workflow contracts, validators, state bindings, cassettes, and cleanup suggestions.
FR7: FR7: User Approval - Generated workflow contracts must be editable and approved before being treated as stable.
FR8: FR8: Capture-Time Redaction - Secrets must be redacted before requests, responses, cassettes, traces, or reports are persisted.
FR9: FR9: Canonical Evidence Schema - Record, replay verify, live verify, eval, validators, and reports must consume canonical evidence events.
FR10: FR10: Replay Verify - Lumyn must support deterministic replay verification without network calls.
FR11: FR11: API State Validation - Lumyn must support `api_state` validators and explicit proof-gap reporting when no reliable read-back exists.
FR12: FR12: Trace Assertions - Lumyn must support a limited deterministic `trace_assertion` vocabulary.
FR13: FR13: Local Trace And Report - Lumyn must produce a local JSON trace and HTML report.
FR14: FR14: Stable CLI Output - Lumyn must support stable exit codes and `--json`.
FR15: FR15: Live Known-Path Verify - Lumyn must support live known-path verification against sandbox.
FR16: FR16: Cleanup And Sandbox Hygiene - Lumyn must support cleanup steps, run IDs, idempotency where practical, and basic orphan detection.
FR17: FR17: Action Boundary - Lumyn must support a first-class action-boundary object and deterministic boundary checks.
FR18: FR18: Boundary Failure Semantics - Lumyn must fail a workflow when expected final state passes but configured action boundaries are violated.

FR19: FR19: Live Agent Eval - Lumyn must support live agent probes using OpenAI-compatible provider adapters, including custom base_url local endpoints, and Anthropic provider adapters behind a pluggable provider interface.
FR20: FR20: Eval Context Modes - Lumyn eval must support `surface_only` and `contract_assisted` modes.
FR21: FR21: Eval Repetition - Lumyn eval must support repeated runs and report pass rate instead of hiding stochastic behavior.
FR22: FR22: Model Pinning - Lumyn must record provider, model, temperature, and model snapshot where available.
FR23: FR23: Baseline Comparison - Lumyn eval must compare against a saved baseline where configured and label drift risk when model snapshots are unavailable.
FR24: FR24: Least-Privilege Agent Runs - Lumyn eval must default to stateless local runs, explicit context, allowlisted env vars, and no ambient workspace access.
FR25: FR25: Portable Artifacts - Workflow contracts, cassettes, traces, reports, and baselines must remain useful outside a hosted service.
NFR1: NFR1: Fast First Value - A technical user should run `init`, `check`, and open bundled traces in under 10 minutes.
NFR2: NFR2: Fast First Workflow - A technical user should record and replay-verify one simple workflow in under 30 minutes.
NFR3: NFR3: Deterministic Replay - Repeated replay verification on unchanged artifacts must produce the same result.
NFR4: NFR4: Proof Honesty - Reports must clearly distinguish confirmed read-back proof, trace-only evidence, and proof gaps.

NFR5: NFR5: Security - Redaction must happen before artifact persistence. If a value cannot be confidently redacted, Lumyn must fail closed: mark the artifact unsafe to persist rather than write a possibly-unredacted secret. Silent persistence of an unrecognized secret is a trust bug, not an edge case.
NFR6: NFR6: Local Privacy - Lumyn must not send specs, docs, traces, cassettes, or API responses to Lumyn by default.
NFR7: NFR7: Contract Readability - Workflow YAML must be understandable by engineers without a custom UI.
NFR8: NFR8: Parser-First - OpenAPI checks should use structured parsers and schema-aware libraries where possible.
NFR9: NFR9: Debuggable Errors - Errors must include file, path, command, object, or line references where possible.
NFR10: NFR10: Cost Visibility - Live evals must show token and estimated cost information.
NFR11: NFR11: Stochastic Honesty - Eval must report run counts and pass rates. It must not hide variability behind a deterministic-looking boolean.
NFR12: NFR12: Artifact Stability - Schemas and artifacts must be versioned.
NFR13: NFR13: Inference Humility - Recorder-generated validators, bindings, boundaries, and cleanup suggestions must be confidence-labeled.
NFR14: NFR14: No Dashboard Dependency - The MVP must explain value through local CLI output and local reports.
REC-QUALITY-001: At least 70% of drafted assertions, read-back candidates, and state bindings are accepted without edit on simple REST/OpenAPI workflows.
RCRR-001: `lumyn init` writes valid config and source-check artifacts.
RCRR-002: `lumyn check` emits a workflow-relevant source finding with a concrete reference.
RCRR-003: `lumyn record create_customer_with_readback` writes workflow YAML, cassette, trace, and state bindings.
RCRR-004: Generated workflow YAML is readable and editable without a custom UI.
RCRR-005: `lumyn verify --strategy replay` is deterministic on repeated runs.
RCRR-006: Strong-proof workflow reports `confirmed by API read-back`.
RCRR-007: Proof-gap workflow reports `not provable from current surface`.
RCRR-008: Local HTML report identifies the fix target.
RCRR-009: Standard auth headers and configured sensitive fields are redacted in all artifacts.
RCRR-010: Bundled static traces open without model key, sandbox, or hosted account.
RCRR-011: `lumyn demo` opens the bundled agent-failure and proof-gap reports with no install, model key, sandbox, or hosted account.
RCRR-012: `lumyn demo` includes bundled examples that show proof-gap and boundary-violation/safety findings without requiring a hosted account, model key, or sandbox.
LVCIS-001: `lumyn verify --strategy live` executes the recorded path against sandbox.
LVCIS-002: Live read-back confirms expected state on the happy path.
LVCIS-003: A changed sandbox response causes a verification failure.
LVCIS-004: Cleanup removes created resources or reports orphaned resources.
LVCIS-005: A forbidden path call fails the workflow even when final state passes.
LVCIS-006: Request and write-operation budgets are enforced.
LVCIS-007: The GitHub Action runs `verify` in CI and posts a PR-comment summary naming changed sources and the workflows they affect.

LVCIS-008: `lumyn share` re-runs redaction at export and produces a single self-contained artifact of a trace that opens with no Lumyn install or account; it fails closed if any value cannot be confidently redacted.
LVCIS-009: Action-boundary violations that indicate scope escalation, data exposure risk, or out-of-policy action are labeled safety/security-relevant and fail even when final state passes.
LVCIS-010: CI, JUnit, and PR-comment output surface completion result, proof strength, boundary status, safety/security finding count, and fix target.
EVAL-001: `lumyn eval` runs OpenAI-compatible provider/model adapters, including custom base_url local endpoints, and Anthropic provider/model adapters when configured.
EVAL-002: Eval supports `surface_only` mode.
EVAL-003: Eval supports `contract_assisted` mode.
EVAL-004: Eval reports run count and pass rate.
EVAL-005: Eval reports selected endpoints and wrong turns.
EVAL-006: Eval reports cost and duration.
EVAL-007: Eval writes a local trace/report.
EVAL-008: Eval compares to a saved baseline where configured.
EVAL-009: Eval labels baseline drift risk when model snapshots are unavailable.
EVAL-010: Eval is diagnostic by default unless an explicit gate is configured.

EVAL-011: Eval emits normalized local failure events for wrong turns, boundary violations, proof gaps, docs/API ambiguity, model-specific failures, stochastic variance, cost/duration, provider/model/eval mode, and fix target.
ACT-001: First trace opened in under 5 minutes.
ACT-002: `init` plus `check` completed in under 10 minutes.
ACT-003: First workflow recorded and replay-verified in under 30 minutes.
ACT-004: Generated workflow contract accepted with limited edits.
PULL-001: User records more than one workflow.
PULL-002: User adds `verify --strategy replay` to CI.
PULL-003: User configures live sandbox verify.
PULL-004: User asks to run live eval before an API/docs release.
PULL-005: User fixes docs, OpenAPI descriptions, auth guidance, endpoint ambiguity, or read-back gaps based on Lumyn output.

CLI-AGENT-001: Agent-facing CLI output supports stable JSON mode, non-interactive or piped output is machine-readable unless the command is explicitly human-only, and quiet/compact modes preserve status, evidence refs, typed exits, and machine-readable errors.

mvp_required_version_slices.name: Record, Contract, Replay, Report
mvp_required_version_slices.completion_policy: Required internal capability slice; not a standalone public release or MVP completion point.

mvp_required_version_slices.name: Live Known-Path Verify, Basic Boundaries, CI, And Share
mvp_required_version_slices.completion_policy: Required internal capability slice; live sandbox/network work remains gated by approved credential posture.

mvp_required_version_slices.name: Live Agent Eval
mvp_required_version_slices.completion_policy: Required internal capability slice; live eval dispatch remains gated by product-pull evidence and approved model/network posture.

delivery_slices.name: Live Known-Path Verify, Basic Boundaries, CI, And Share

proof_of_behavior_requirements.rule: Tasks must close at or above their required_proof_level; workflow or user-visible closure requires proof-of-behavior scorecard evidence or approved exception.
